lines.en_lit: to enter directly, And Rabbi Yosei said to you: The Jewish people are beloved as the Torah did not require them of an agent,
But Rabbi Yehuda, too, let enter between candelabrum and the wall. blacken his clothes
Rabbi Natan said: one-cubit partition, the Sages did not determine whether of the inside or of the outside

Ravina strongly objects to this What is the reason If we say because it is written: “And the House that King Solomon built for the Lord was sixty cubits in length, and its width was twenty cubits, and its height was thirty cubits” and it is written: “And the House, that is, the Sanctuary, was forty cubits long” and it is written: “And before the partition twenty cubits in length, and twenty cubits in width, and twenty cubits in height” and we do not know whether the one-cubit partition of these twenty of these forty
But perhaps not of these twenty nor of these forty counts the space does not count walls Know as anywhere that counts the walls counts

As we learned The Sanctuary one hundred by one hundred with a height of one hundred wall of the Entrance Hall five and the Entrance Hall eleven wall of the Sanctuary six length forty cubits; and one-cubit partition; Hall of Holy of Holies twenty cubits; wall of the Sanctuary six and the chamber six and the wall of the chamber five
Rather, its sanctity, whether like the inside or like the outside

And this is what Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Yosef of Hutzal raised a dilemma “And he prepared a partition in the midst of the House within to set there the Ark of the Covenant of the Lord” dilemma was raised before what is speaking?
And prepared a partition in the midst of the House within to set there, Or perhaps this is states: A partition in the midst of the House within,
And was uncertain But wasn’t it taught Isi ben Yehuda says: five verses in the Torah whose cannot be decided,
